Default value if specified via `@defaultValue`.

#### CkmManifest

CkmManifest

The complete Codebase Knowledge Manifest.

Generated by `forge-ts build` from TSDoc annotations. Consumed by CLI tools for structured help output.

Members:

##### concepts

CkmConcept[]

Domain concepts (interfaces, types).

##### operations

CkmOperation[]

User-facing operations (exported functions).

##### constraints

CkmConstraint[]

Enforced rules.

##### workflows

CkmWorkflow[]

Multi-step workflows.

##### configSchema

CkmConfigEntry[]

Configuration schema entries.

#### CkmTopic

CkmTopic

An auto-generated topic derived from CKM data.

Members:

##### name

string

Topic slug used as CLI argument (e.g., `'calver'`).

##### summary

string

One-line summary.

##### concepts

CkmConcept[]

Related concept names from the CKM.

##### operations

CkmOperation[]

Related operations from the CKM.

##### configSchema

CkmConfigEntry[]

Related config entries from the CKM.

##### constraints

CkmConstraint[]

Related constraints from the CKM.

#### CkmEngine

CkmEngine

The CKM engine — provides topic derivation, filtering, and output formatting.

Instantiate via `createCkmEngine`. All topic data is derived from the CKM manifest at construction time with zero configuration.

Members:

##### topics

CkmTopic[]

All auto-derived topics.

##### getTopicIndex()

(toolName?: string) => string

Returns a formatted topic index for terminal display.

Parameters:
- toolName: CLI tool name for the usage line.

Returns: Formatted string.

##### getTopicContent()

(topicName: string) => string | null

Returns human-readable content for a topic.

Parameters:
- topicName: Topic slug.

Returns: Formatted content, or null if not found.

##### getTopicJson()

(topicName?: string) => object

Returns CKM-filtered JSON for a topic (machine-readable).

Parameters:
- topicName: Topic slug, or undefined for full index.

Returns: JSON-serializable object.

##### getManifest()

() => CkmManifest

Returns the raw CKM manifest.

#### Suggestion

Suggestion

Feedback entry point exports for suggestion and guidance helpers.

Members:

##### message

string

Human-readable guidance for the user.

##### fix

string | undefined

Command or action text that can be used to address the issue.

##### autoFixable

boolean

Indicates whether VersionGuard can apply the suggestion automatically.

#### FeedbackResult

FeedbackResult

Aggregates validation errors with suggested next steps.

Members:

##### valid

boolean

Indicates whether the inspected version state is valid.

##### errors

ValidationError[]

Validation errors collected during the check.

##### suggestions

Suggestion[]

Suggested next steps for resolving the reported issues.

##### canAutoFix

boolean

Indicates whether at least one suggestion can be auto-applied.

#### VersionSourceProvider

VersionSourceProvider

Abstraction for reading and writing a version string from any manifest format.

Members:

##### name

string

Human-readable provider name (e.g. `'package.json'`, `'Cargo.toml'`).

##### manifestFile

string

Default manifest filename this provider handles.

##### exists()

(cwd: string) => boolean

Returns `true` when the manifest file exists in `cwd`.

##### getVersion()

(cwd: string) => string

Reads the version string from the manifest. Throws if missing or unreadable.

##### setVersion()

(version: string, cwd: string) => void

Writes a version string back to the manifest. Throws if the file does not exist.

#### PackageJsonObject

PackageJsonObject

Recursive object type used for arbitrary JSON-compatible package values.

#### PackageJson

PackageJson

Minimal shape of a `package.json` document used by VersionGuard.

Members:

##### name

string | undefined

Package name.

##### version

string | undefined

Package version string.

#### FixResult

FixResult

Fix entry point exports for auto-remediation helpers.

Members:

##### fixed

boolean

Indicates whether the operation changed repository state.

##### message

string

Human-readable description of the fix attempt.

##### file

string | undefined

Absolute path to the file that was updated, when applicable.

#### ProjectRootResult

ProjectRootResult

Result of project root detection.

Members:

##### found

boolean

Whether a project root was found.

##### root

string

The resolved project root directory, or the original cwd if not found.

##### marker

string | undefined

Which marker file was found.

##### hasConfig

boolean

Whether the directory has a VersionGuard config.

##### hasGit

boolean

Whether the directory is inside a git repository.

##### hasManifest

boolean

Whether a version manifest file exists.

#### TagInfo

TagInfo

Tag entry point exports for release-tag management helpers.

Members:

##### name

string

Full git tag name, including any prefix such as `v`.

##### version

string

Normalized version string derived from the tag name.

##### message

string | undefined

Annotated tag message when one is available.

##### date

Date

Timestamp associated with the tag lookup result.

#### InitOptions

InitOptions

Options for headless (non-interactive) initialization.

Members:

##### cwd

string

Working directory path.

##### type

"semver" | "calver" | undefined

Versioning type (semver or calver).

##### format

string | undefined

CalVer format string.

##### allowVPrefix

boolean | undefined

Allow v-prefix on SemVer versions.

##### allowBuildMetadata

boolean | undefined

Allow build metadata on SemVer versions.

##### requirePrerelease

boolean | undefined

Require prerelease labels on SemVer versions.

##### manifest

string | undefined

Manifest source type.

##### github

boolean | undefined

Whether to generate GitHub integration files (dependabot.yml).

##### hooks

boolean | undefined

Whether to install git hooks.

##### changelog

boolean | undefined

Whether to enable changelog validation.

##### yes

boolean | undefined

Accept defaults without prompting.

##### force

boolean | undefined

Overwrite existing config.

### Types

#### VersioningType

VersioningType

Supported versioning strategies.

#### ManifestSourceType

ManifestSourceType

Supported manifest source types for version extraction.

#### CalVerToken

CalVerToken

Valid CalVer token names for building format strings.

#### CalVerFormat

CalVerFormat

A CalVer format string composed of dot-separated tokens.

Any dot-separated combination of valid `CalVerToken` values is accepted. Common examples: `'YYYY.MM.MICRO'`, `'YY.0M.MICRO'`, `'YYYY.0M.0D'`, `'YYYY.MM.DD.MICRO'`.  `MICRO` and `PATCH` are interchangeable — both represent a 0-based incrementing counter. The CalVer specification (calver.org) uses `MICRO`; `PATCH` is accepted as a SemVer-familiar alias.

#### ValidateMode

ValidateMode

Controls whether validate runs all checks or a fast subset.

#### ParsedVersion

ParsedVersion

Union of supported parsed version payloads.

#### PackageJsonValue

PackageJsonValue

JSON-compatible scalar, array, or object value used by package metadata.

#### PackageJsonArray

PackageJsonArray

Recursive array type used for arbitrary JSON-compatible package values.

### Variables

#### MANIFEST_TO_ECOSYSTEM

Record<ManifestSourceType, string | null>

Maps VersionGuard manifest source types to Dependabot package-ecosystem values.

Returns `null` for sources that have no Dependabot equivalent (VERSION files, git tags, custom regex). The `auto` source is resolved at detection time, not mapped directly.

#### REGISTRY_TABLE

Record<string, { registry: string; check: (packageName: string, version: string, config: PublishConfig) => Promise<PublishCheckResult> | PublishCheckResult; }>

Maps manifest source types to their registry check implementations.

Each entry provides the registry name and a check function that returns whether the given version is published. The check function receives the package name, version, and publish config.
